summarization.a: The multiple line chart shows the deposition rate (Å/cycle) of  Zirconia (ZrO₂ ) thin films from two different zirconium precursors—(CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me and (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂—using O₃ as the oxidant, plotted against deposition temperature (250–500 °C).  The growth rate seems to increase with increase in temperature and the behaviours are slightly different for the two precursors.

data_extraction.a:
| Deposition Temperature (°C) | Growth Rate (Å/cycle) | Process   |
|-----------------------------|------------------------|-----------------------------|
| 250                         | 0.24                   | (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me / O₃       |
| 250                         | 0.21                   | (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ / O₃           |
| 300                         | 0.56                   | (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me / O₃       |
| 300                         | 0.52                   | (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ / O₃           |
| 350                         | 0.64                   | (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me / O₃       |
| 350                         | 0.56                   | (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ / O₃           |
| 400                         | 0.75                   | (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me / O₃       |
| 400                         | 0.58                   | (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ / O₃           |
| 450                         | 0.88                   | (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me / O₃       |
| 450                         | 0.71                   | (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ / O₃           |
| 500                         | 1.10                   | (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me / O₃       |
| 500                         | —                      | (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ / O₃           |

a.question: List out the two precursors used for the deposition in this study and what where the experimental conditions for the deposition cycle?
a.answer:
1. Precursors Used:

(CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me

(CpMe)₂ZrMe₂

2. Experimental Conditions for One ALD Cycle:

Zr Precursor Pulse Time: 1.0 s

O₃ (Oxidant) Pulse Time: 2.0 s

Deposition Temperature Range: 250–500 °C

Oxidant: O₃ (Ozone)

a.question: Compare the thermal ALD windows of the two precursors. What is the main requiste in choosing (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me over (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ for low-temperature processing?
a.answer: (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me provides a stable, low deposition rate (~0.5 Å/cycle) from 250–400 °C, while (CpMe)₂ZrMe₂ only achieves a stable rate above 350 °C. The balance is between low-temperature capability and growth rate. Choosing the (OMe)Me precursor enables processing below 350 °C, but at the cost of a roughly 2x slower growth rate compared to the Me₂ precursor at its optimal temperature.

a.question: How does changing the ligand from -Me to -OMe in the precursor structure affect the deposition rate at 300 °C?
a.answer: It reduces the deposition rate by approximately half (from ~1.0 Å/cycle to ~0.5 Å/cycle).

a.question: If a ZrO₂ film is needed for a back-end-of-line (BEOL) capacitor where thermal input is limited to 300 °C, which precursor must be used and what is a likely consequence for manufacturing?
a.answer: The (CpMe)₂Zr(OMe)Me precursor must be used, as it shows a stable rate at 300 °C. The consequence is a slower growth rate, which will increase the total deposition time per wafer to achieve the target film thickness. This reduces tool throughput and increases the cost of the manufacturing step.
